utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Provided, but split into health_coverage and mental_health_coverage. The required item is 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'. The plan lists primary/spouse/children plans with monthly costs, and a separate mental_health_coverage with monthly cost. Together, this covers both physical and mental health with costs, so counts as included. 2) property_coverage: Required 'home/contents insurance with costs'. The plan has property_coverage: null. Not included. 3) travel_coverage: Required 'travel insurance with costs'. The plan lists features only, no costs. Not fully specified, so not included. 4) additional_services: Required 'any enhancement services with costs'. The plan lists services but no costs. Not fully specified, so not included. 5) total_budget: Required 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. The plan provides monthly_total and annual_total (and a savings figure). This is included. Therefore, included items: health_coverage and total_budget = 2 out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes the following named items that appear in the ground-truth ratings: EliteCare Premium Plan (primary health), HealthFirst Standard Plan (spouse health), LifeFirst Standard Plan (children health), and a travel coverage section without a plan name (so no mapping possible). The mental health plan listed is 'Expatriate Wellness Mental Health Package' which does not exist in the ground-truth, so it cannot be mapped. Property coverage is null. Therefore, only the three health plan names can be mapped to their ratings from the primary_health_coverage.physical_health section.
